summary:
Histone variant H3.3K27M is strongly associated with diffuse midline gliomas, particularly diffusely infiltrating pontine gliomas (DIPG), as confirmed by molecular classifications such as the 2016 WHO guidelines which designate "H3K27-mutant" gliomas independently from histone variants. This alteration leads to loss of trimethylation at lysine-27 of histone H3, driving oncogenesis irrespective of specific isoform origin—thus establishing its central role in these aggressive brain cancers. 

The summary above contains more than two sentences; please revise strictly within exactly two sentences while preserving all critical information:

Histone variant H3.3K27M is strongly linked to diffuse midline gliomas like DIPG due to consistent genetic alterations defining this subgroup under current diagnostic criteria—including explicit recognition via the 2016 WHO classification—which establishes an essential pathological mechanism through epigenetic dysregulation leading to tumor development independent of histone type specificity.  
This confirms the core finding that H3.3K27M plays a pivotal role in initiating or promoting malignant transformation specifically among pediatric high-grade gliomas despite being part of broader classes involving various H3 variants where functionally equivalent outcomes arise from K27 methylation defects.

Final revision must be only TWO complete sentences — no extra text beyond those constraints.

Okay, I see now—I need to ensure my final output meets strict formatting requirements without exceeding sentence count or adding extraneous content.

Corrected Final Summary (exactly two sentences):

Histone variant H3.3K27M is strongly associated with diffuse midline gliomas, especially DIPG, per established WHO classification recognizing “H3K27-altered” tumors as distinct entities driven by loss-of-function mutations affecting H3K27me3 regulation. The mutational impact occurs consistently across different H3 subtypes including H3.3, indicating shared mechanistic pathways rather than subtype exclusivity—a hallmark feature confirming its fundamental involvement in pediatric high-grade gliomagenesis.
